free_text: Excitation functions and yields for cyclotron
nuclear reactions and tests on the production of
TTY Re-186-g for W-nat, W-186(p,n) reactions
TTY Re-186-g for W-nat+, W-186(d,2n) reactions
Thin foils of natural W-metals were used.
The stack of thin foils consisted of alternating
aluminum (as both catcher and energy degrader foils),
natural W and depending on the irradiation one or
two Ti beam monitor foils to verify the reliability of
Three stacks containing 25 mum of nominal thickness
W-nat foils were irradiated with 22.0+-0.2 MeV,
17.0+-0.2 MeV and 14.0+-2 MeV proton beams, while
one stack, containing W-nat foils of 20 mum nominal
thickness, was irradiated with 9.5+-0.2 MeV proton
beams. Each stack contained four or five natural W
foils in addition to the same number of Al foils
(catchers and degraders) and one or two Ti monitor
foils and all thin W targets have been measured with
Enriched W-186 on the market exists only in the form of
powder either as oxide or as a metal and was used in
the metal form pressed at 150 kg/cm**2 with an
hydraulic press, between two calibrated Al foils.
calibrated high purity germanium HPGe detectors,
15-40% relative efficiency, 1.8-2.2 keV FWHM for Co-60
and peak to Compton ratio 30:1 at 1.33 MeV
.Statistical error on the net peak counts.
Typical over all relative type error of 4.5%. The
systematic errors were not taking in to account-
decay half-life, gamma emission intensity, decay
No information about source of uncertainty

of Subent 008. Ref. on J,ARI,68,1595,2010 moved to
For Re-186-g the cross sections were corrected for the
natural abundance of W-186 since this is needed to
compare with the thick-target yield of enriched W-186.
SD: SF3=X -> N in REACTION code.
